evidence: No @Deprecated annotations anywhere in the production source tree.
GrammarConfigLoader.validateNoLegacyKeys() actively rejects stale config keys ('copulas') with a clear error message — completed migration, not a shim.

The legacy route /api/semantic-field (registered in WordSketchApiServer) maps to handleSemanticFieldComparison alongside the canonical /api/semantic-field/compare. This is an intentional backward-compatible alias documented in both the handler Javadoc and the server endpoint listing.

HttpApiUtils.getCorsAllowOrigin() is a private static method that does nothing but return the CORS_ALLOW_ORIGIN static final field. This method appears to be a vestige from when the field was dynamic; now that it is a static final constant, the method wrapping it adds indirection without purpose. It is called only by setCorsHeader(), which is itself private — the redundancy is confined to a single class.
No mixed JS/TS, no old+new API coexistence beyond the documented legacy route alias.
ExploreResponseAssembler was recently moved from api/ to model/ (recent change noted in task context); the migration appears complete — api/ holds no residual reference to the old location.

strengths: No @Deprecated symbols are called by any active code.
GrammarConfigLoader explicitly validates and rejects stale config schema keys, showing completed migration of the config format.
The legacy /api/semantic-field route alias is documented as such and shares an implementation with the canonical path — no dead code path.
Recent refactors (ExploreResponseAssembler move, Edge.toMap() removal) are fully completed with no orphaned stubs.
incomplete_migration.issue_character: The only vestige is a private getCorsAllowOrigin() wrapper method that indirects over a static final constant; it is confined to one class and has no external callers.
incomplete_migration.score_rationale: The codebase is extremely clean of migration artifacts. All identified refactors mentioned in the change log are complete and leave no stale shims. The legacy route alias is intentional and documented rather than accidentally preserved. The only minor issue (the getCorsAllowOrigin() method) is local and harmless. This places the score well above the midpoint but short of near-perfect, since the vestigial private method slightly reduces clarity.

issues.summary: getCorsAllowOrigin() private method indirects over a static final constant without adding value
evidence: CORS_ALLOW_ORIGIN is declared 'private static final String' initialized once at class load.
getCorsAllowOrigin() is a private method that does only 'return CORS_ALLOW_ORIGIN'.
setCorsHeader() calls getCorsAllowOrigin() rather than referencing the field directly.
The original intent (dynamic configuration) was eliminated when the field was made static final, but the getter was not removed.
issues.suggestion: Remove getCorsAllowOrigin() and have setCorsHeader() reference CORS_ALLOW_ORIGIN directly: exchange.getResponseHeaders().set("Access-Control-Allow-Origin", CORS_ALLOW_ORIGIN);

root_causes: Recent refactoring (static final caching of CORS) removed the justification for the private getter but left the wrapper in place.
possible_false_positives: The /api/semantic-field legacy route may appear as an incomplete migration but is explicitly documented as an intentional backward-compatible alias.
